examples.japanese: 今回の試験はいちばん難しいものを除いてすべての問題に答えた。
examples.english: On this exam I was able to answer every question other than the most difficult one.
examples.pronunciation: konkai no shiken wa ichiban muzukashii mono o nozoite subete no mondai ni kotaeta.

examples.japanese: この仕事は楽だし、給料もいいし、通勤時間が長いことを除けば文句ない。
examples.english: This job is easy and the salary is good. I have no complaints except that the working hours are too long.
examples.pronunciation: kono shigoto wa raku da shi, kyuuryou mo ii shi, tsuukin jikan ga nagai koto o nozokeba monku nai.

meaning: more or less; pretty much; roughly; tentatively ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 一応

examples.japanese: 一応やりました。
examples.english: I did it (but it may still need some work).
examples.pronunciation: ichiou yarimashita.

examples.japanese: 一応やってみます。
examples.english: (I may not be able to do it well, but) I will give it a shot.
examples.pronunciation: ichiou yatte miru.

examples.japanese: 一応、付き合っている人がいるんだ。
examples.english: I am sort of seeing someone..
examples.pronunciation: ichiou, tsukiatteiru hito ga iru nda.

examples.japanese: 彼はもう寝たかもしれないけど、一応電話してみようかな。
examples.english: He may already be asleep, but I guess I should try to give him a call..
examples.pronunciation: kare wa mou neta kamoshirenai kedo, ichiou denwa shite miyou kana.

examples.japanese: 一応、明日までに返事をくれる？
examples.english: Can you at least give me an answer by tomorrow?
examples.pronunciation: ichiou, ashita made ni henji o kureru.

examples.japanese: 一応、連絡先を教えてくれる？
examples.english: Can you give me your contact information just in case?
examples.pronunciation: ichiou, renraku saki o oshiete kureru?

examples.japanese: あの事件は一応片がついているはずだ。
examples.english: That matter has been settled (though maybe not satisfactorily).
examples.pronunciation: ano jiken wa ichiou kata ga tsuiteiru hazu da.

examples.japanese: 念のため一応書類を調べてみて下さい。
examples.english: Just to be sure, please check through the documents.
examples.pronunciation: nen no tame ichiou shorui o shirabete mite kudasai.

meaning: on one hand, on the other hand; although ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: 普通形 + 一方で
usageJapanese:
①Xは〜だが、Yは〜（対比）
②ある面では〜だが、別の面では〜（並列）

examples.japanese: お母さんは優しい一方で、お父さんはこわい。
examples.english: My mother is kind, but on the other hand my father is scary.
examples.pronunciation: okaasan wa yasashii ippou de, otousan wa kowai.

examples.japanese: 父は自分に厳しい一方で、他人には優しい。
examples.english: My father is very strict with himself but very kind to others.
examples.pronunciation: chichi wa jibun ni kibishii ippou de, ta’nin niwa yasashii.

examples.japanese: 彼女の仕事は夏は非常に忙しい一方、冬は暇になる。
examples.english: Her work is very busy in the summer, but on the other hand it is much more relaxed in the winter.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo no shigoto wa natsu wa hijou ni isogashii ippou, fuyu wa hima ni naru.

examples.japanese: 彼は俳優である一方で、歌手としても活躍している。
examples.english: He is an actor, and on the other hand is active as a singer.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa haiyuu dearu ippou de, kashu toshite mo katsuyaku shite iru.

examples.japanese: 海外旅行は非日常を体験できることから、楽しいと感じることが多い一方で、不安なこともある。
examples.english: Traveling abroad can be extraordinary, so it's often fun, but on the other hand can be uneasy.
examples.pronunciation: kaigai ryokou wa hinichijou o taiken dekiru koto kara, tanoshii to kanjiru koto ga ooi ippou de, fuan na koto mo aru.

examples.japanese: その仕事はあまり面白くなかったが、その一方で給与はよかった。
examples.english: That job was not very interesting, but on the other hand it paid well.
examples.pronunciation: sono shigoto wa amari omoshiroku nakatta ga, sono ippou de kyuuyo wa yokatta.

examples.japanese: 老人の人口が増えている一方で、若年労働力の数がどんどん減っています。
examples.english: While the number of old people is increasing, the young labor force is decreasing.
examples.pronunciation: roujin no jinkou ga fueteiru ippou de, jakunen roudouryoku no kazu ga dondon hette imasu.

meaning: in one go; without stopping; all at once; immediately; instantly; right away ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 一気に

examples.japanese: お茶を一気に飲みました。
examples.english: I drank all of the tea at once.
examples.pronunciation: ocha o ikki ni nomimashita.

examples.japanese: 私はこの本を一気に読みました。
examples.english: I read this book all in one go.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa kono hon o ikki ni yomimashita.

examples.japanese: 彼は一気に１０時間働いた。
examples.english: He worked for 10 hours without stopping.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa ikki ni juu jikan hataraita.

examples.japanese: こういう仕事は一気にやった方がいいんだよ。
examples.english: This kind of work is best done all at once.
examples.pronunciation: kouiu shigoto wa ikki ni yatta hou ga ii nda yo.

examples.japanese: 買ってきたお菓子は一気に食べないで、少しずつ食べましょうね。
examples.english: Don't eat all of the sweets you bought in one go, let's take our time eating them.
examples.pronunciation: katte kita okashi wa ikki ni tabenaide, sukoshi zutsu tabemashou ne.

examples.japanese: その仕事を一気にやってしまいなさい。
examples.english: Please finish that job immediately.
examples.pronunciation: sono shigoto o ikki ni yatte shimai nasai.

examples.japanese: 値段が一気に高くなっている。
examples.english: The price suddenly jumped high.
examples.pronunciation: nedan ga ikki ni takaku natteiru.

examples.japanese: 彼の日本語は一気に上達した。
examples.english: His Japanese improved in no time.
examples.pronunciation: kare no nihongo wa ikki ni joutatsu shita.

meaning: now that; since; as long as ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: V（辞書形 / タ形） + 上は
usageJapanese: 〜なのだから、当然・・・。
usageNotes:
①かなり硬い表現
②追い詰められた状況、決意・覚悟を言う時に使われる。

examples.japanese: 約束した上は、どんなことがあっても守ります。
examples.english: Since I’ve made a promise, I will keep it no matter what happens.
examples.pronunciation: yakusoku shita ue wa, donna koto ga atte mo mamorimasu.

examples.japanese: プロ選手になると決めた上は、苦しくても頑張らなければならない。
examples.english: Since you've decided to become a professional athlete, even if things get tough you have to always do your best.
examples.pronunciation: puro senshu ni naru to kimeta ue wa, kurushiku temo ganbaranakereba naranai.

examples.japanese: N2を受験する上は、高得点で合格したいです。
examples.english: Since I will be taking the N2, I want to pass with a high score.
examples.pronunciation: N2 o juken suru ue wa, koutokuten de goukaku shitai desu.

examples.japanese: 家族を離れて日本で働く上は、十分な覚悟が必要である。
examples.english: Since you will be moving away from your family to work in Japan, you will need to properly prepare yourself.
examples.pronunciation: kazoku o hanarete nihon de hataraku ue wa, juubun na kakugo ga hitsuyou de aru.

examples.japanese: 彼は仕事を引き受けた上はいつも全力を尽くします。
examples.english: He always does his best after taking a job.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa shigoto o hikiuketa ue wa itsumo zenryoku o tsukushimasu.

examples.japanese: このプロジェクトは失敗した。こうなった上は言い訳をしても無駄だ。
examples.english: This project failed. Since it's come to this, there is no point in making excuses.
examples.pronunciation: kono purojekuto wa shippai shita. kounatta ue wa iiwake o shitemo muda da.

examples.japanese: 留学を決心した上は、仕事を頑張って留学の代金を貯金すべきだ。
examples.english: Since I've decided to study abroad, I need to work hard and save up money for the trip.
examples.pronunciation: ryuugaku o kesshin shita ue wa, shigoto o ganbatte ryuugaku no daikin o chokin subeki da.

examples.japanese: 高いお金を払って旅行する上は、たくさんの場所を観光して、その土地のおいしい物を食べたいです。
examples.english: Since I spend a lot of money for travelling, I want to sightsee at many places and eat delicious local foods.
examples.pronunciation: takai okane o haratte ryokou suru ue wa, takusan no basho o kankou shite, sono tochi no oishii mono o tabetai desu.

meaning: for the sake of; from the standpoint of; as a matter of; in terms of ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
普通形 + 上に
・V（普）+上に
・イA（普） + 上に
・ナAな + 上に
・ナAである + 上に
・Nの + 上に
・Nである + 上に
usageJapanese: ～に加えて / それに
usageNotes: 「上に」の前が良いことの場合、「上に」の後ろも良いことが来る。逆に「上に」の前が悪いことの場合、「上に」の後ろも悪いことが来る。

examples.japanese: 健康上ではポテトフライや揚げ物などは食べない方がいいと思います。
examples.english: For the sake of one's health, I think it's best to not eat french fries or other fried foods.
examples.pronunciation: kenkou jou de wa poteto furai ya agemono nado wa tabenai hou ga ii to omoi masu.

examples.japanese: 日本の法律上はお酒を飲んで車を運転するのは禁止です。
examples.english: In terms of Japanese law, it is illegal to drink and drive.
examples.pronunciation: nihon no houritsu jou wa osake o nonde kuruma o unten suru no wa kinshi desu.

examples.japanese: 理論上はこれが可能だが、実用化には時間がかかりそうだ。
examples.english: In theory this is possible, but to actually implement it looks like it will take some time.
examples.pronunciation: riron jou wa kore ga kanou da ga, jitsuyouka ni wa jikan ga kakari sou da.

examples.japanese: 彼女は健康上の理由で仕事を辞めたそうです。
examples.english: She apparently quit her job for the sake of her health.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wakenkou jou no riyuu de shigoto o yameta sou desu.

examples.japanese: 会社の規則上は作業中にヘルメットを必ず被ることです。
examples.english: In accordance with the company rules, one must always wear their helmet during work.
examples.pronunciation: kaisha no kisoku jou wa sagyouchuu ni herumetto o kanarazu kaburu koto desu.

examples.japanese: この番組は教育上は良くないから、子供に見せない方がいい。
examples.english: This program is not good for the sake of education, so it's best to not show it to your kids.
examples.pronunciation: kono bangumi wa kyouiku jou wa yokunai kara, kodomo ni misenai hou ga ii.

examples.japanese: 僕たちは結婚式をしませんが、法律上は結婚しています。
examples.english: We will not have a wedding ceremony, but in the eyes of the law we are married.
examples.pronunciation: bokutachi wa kekkonshiki o shimasen ga, houritsu jou wa kekkon shite imasu.

id: grammar:n2:中を / 中では（なかを / なかでは）
word: 中を / 中では（なかを / なかでは）
meaning: in; on; in the midst of; when; while ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
N / phrase / sentence + 中を
Fixed expression / pattern: 中では（なかを
Fixed expression / pattern: なかでは）
connectionSourcePoint: 中を / 中では（なかを / なかでは）

examples.japanese: 私はお店の中をうろうろしていた。
examples.english: I wandered around in the store.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa omise no naka o urouro shite ita.

examples.japanese: 雨が降っているから彼女は部屋の中を走った。
examples.english: Since it was raining outside, she ran inside.
examples.pronunciation: ame ga futte iru kara kanojo wa heya no naka o hashitta.

examples.japanese: 彼らは雨の中を歩き続けました。
examples.english: They continued to walk in the rain.
examples.pronunciation: karera wa ame no naka o aruki tsuduke mashita.

examples.japanese: 教会の中では帽子を脱ぎなさい。
examples.english: Please remove your hat inside the church.
examples.pronunciation: kyoukai no naka dewa boushi o nugi nasai.

examples.japanese: 携帯電話は電車の中では使わない方がいい。
examples.english: You had best not use your cellphone on the train.
examples.pronunciation: keitai denwa wa densha no naka dewa tsukawanai hou ga ii.

examples.japanese: お忙しい中を手伝っていただき、本当にありがとうございます。
examples.english: Thank you very much for helping me while you are busy.
examples.pronunciation: oisogashii naka o tetsudatte itadaki, hontou ni arigatou gozaimasu.

examples.japanese: 地元のビールの中では、どれがおいしいですか。
examples.english: What's the best local beer that you recommend?
examples.pronunciation: jimoto no biiru no naka dewa, dore ga oishii desu ka?

examples.japanese: 私の聞いた中では、今までで最高の演奏だった。
examples.english: That was the best musical performance I’ve ever heard.
examples.pronunciation: watashi no kiita naka dewa, ima made de saikou no ensou datta.

meaning: more than; not less than; beyond ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 以上に

examples.japanese: 今まで以上に仕事を頑張ります。
examples.english: I will work even harder at work than I have until now.
examples.pronunciation: ima made ijou ni shigoto o ganbarimasu.

examples.japanese: 必要以上にたくさんのお金を使わないでください。
examples.english: Please don’t use more money than you need.
examples.pronunciation: hitsuyou ijou ni takusan no okane o tsukawanaide kudasai.

examples.japanese: 私が予想していた以上に彼の様態はよくなかった。
examples.english: His condition was worse than I had expected.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa yosou shite ita ijou ni kare no youtai wa yokunakatta.

examples.japanese: 今以上に日本語ができるようになりたいです。
examples.english: I want to get even better at Japanese than I am now.
examples.pronunciation: ima ijou ni nihongo ga dekiru youni naritai desu.

examples.japanese: 私たちが泊まったホテルは、予想以上に豪華だった。
examples.english: The hotel that we stayed in was even more extravagant than we had imagined.
examples.pronunciation: watashi tachi ga tomatta hoteru wa, yosou ijou ni gouka datta.

examples.japanese: 彼らは、わたしが考えた以上に強い反応を示した。
examples.english: They were taking the news worse than i had expected.
examples.pronunciation: karera wa, watashi ga kangaeta ijou ni tsuyoi hannou o shimeshita.

examples.japanese: この技術はあなたが思っている以上に注目されている。
examples.english: This technology is attracting more attention than you think it is.
examples.pronunciation: kono gijutsu wa anata ga omotte iru ijou ni chuumoku sarete iru.

examples.japanese: あなたは私が思っている以上にとても素敵な人でした。
examples.english: You were an even more wonderful person than I had thought.
examples.pronunciation: anata wa watashi ga omotte iru ijou ni totemo suteki na hito deshita.

meaning: because; since; seeing that ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
V（普通形） + 以上（は）
ナAである + 以上（は）
Nである + 以上（は）
usageJapanese: 〜なのだから、当然・・・。

examples.japanese: 約束した以上、きちんと守ってくださいね。
examples.english: You've made a promise, so be sure to keep it!
examples.pronunciation: yakusoku shita ijou, kichinto mamotte kudasai ne.

examples.japanese: 学生である以上、アルバイトではなく勉強が大事ですよ。
examples.english: Because you are a student, your studies are more important than your part-time job.
examples.pronunciation: gakusei de aru ijou, arubaito dewa naku benkyou ga daiji desu yo.

examples.japanese: 給料がこんなに安い以上は、転職を考える人がいるのも当然だ。
examples.english: With such low salaries, it's no wonder there are people who want to change jobs.
examples.pronunciation: kyuuryou ga konna ni yasui ijou wa, tenshoku o kangaeru hito ga iru no mo touzen da.

examples.japanese: お金を払って勉強する以上は、しっかり身につけたい。
examples.english: Since I am paying money to study, I really want to learn.
examples.pronunciation: okane o haratte benkyou suru ijou wa, shikkari mi ni tsuketai.

examples.japanese: 一度やると決めた以上は、最後まで責任を持ってやらないと。
examples.english: Once you decide to do something, you must stay responsible until the end.
examples.pronunciation: ichido yaru to kimeta ijou wa, saigo made sekinin o motte yaranai to.

examples.japanese: 出場が決まった以上、いい結果が出せるように頑張ろうと思う。
examples.english: Since my participation has been confirmed, I want to work hard to get a good result.
examples.pronunciation: shutsujou ga kimatta ijou, ii kekka ga daseru you ni ganbarou to omou.

examples.japanese: JLPTに申し込んだ以上、しっかり勉強して合格したい。
examples.english: Because I've applied for the JLPT, I want to study hard and pass.
examples.pronunciation: JLPT ni moushi konda ijou, shikkari benkyou shite goukaku shitai.

examples.japanese: 会社を辞めると決めた以上、早く上司にその旨を伝えなければならない。
examples.english: Since I've decided to quit my job, I need to hurry up and report that to my boss.
examples.pronunciation: kaisha o yameru to kimeta ijou, hayaku joushi ni sono uma o tsutaenakereba naranai.

examples.japanese: 舞台の主役をもらった以上は、最高の演技ができるように頑張ります。
examples.english: Since I've received the lead role for the play, I will do my best to deliver the best performance.
examples.pronunciation: butai no shuyaku o moratta ijou wa, saikou no enki ga dekiru youni ganbarimasu.

meaning: with the exception of; excepting ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 以外

examples.japanese: 食事以外には何ができる？
examples.english: Apart from eating, what else can we do?
examples.pronunciation: shokuji igai ni wa nani ga dekiru?

examples.japanese: 彼女以外誰もいない。
examples.english: There's no one else but her.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo igai dare mo inai.

examples.japanese: 水以外何も見えない。
examples.english: I don't see anything other than water.
examples.pronunciation: mizu igai nanimo mienai.

examples.japanese: 彼は英語以外の言語は全く知らない。
examples.english: He knows no languages other than English.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa eigo igai no gengo wa mattaku shiranai.

examples.japanese: 私は彼女以外はみんな好きだ。
examples.english: I like everyone aside from her.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa kanojo igai wa minna suki da.

examples.japanese: 仕事以外のことも話せてよかったです。
examples.english: I was glad to be able to talk about something other than work.
examples.pronunciation: shigoto igai no koto mo hanasete yokatta desu.

examples.japanese: 変化以外に永久のものはない。
examples.english: There is nothing permanent except change.
examples.pronunciation: henka igai ni eikyuu no mono wa nai.

examples.japanese: 私は中国語以外の言語だと、英語と日本語を話せます。
examples.english: Aside from the Chinese language, I can also speak English and Japanese.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa chuugokugo igai no gengo da to, eigo to nihongo o hanasemasu.

meaning: nothing; (not) ~ at all; there’s no need to ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: 何も + ... + ない

examples.japanese: 私達はなにもいらない。
examples.english: We don't need anything.
examples.pronunciation: watashi tachi wa nani mo iranai.

examples.japanese: 彼女はなにも言わなかった。
examples.english: She did not say anything.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wa nani mo iwana katta.

examples.japanese: どのみち、何も聞こえなかった。
examples.english: In any case, I couldn't hear anything.
examples.pronunciation: dono michi, nanimo kikoenakatta.

examples.japanese: 彼はただ新聞を読むだけでなにもしなかった。
examples.english: He did nothing but read newspapers.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa tada shinbun o yomu dake de nani mo shina katta.

examples.japanese: そのお店は混雑していたので、私はなにも買えなかった。
examples.english: That store was crowded so I couldn't buy anything.
examples.pronunciation: sono omise wa konran shite ita node, watashi wa nani mo kaena katta.

examples.japanese: それは冗談だけなんだから、なにもそんなに怒ることはない。
examples.english: It's just a joke, there’s no need to get so angry.
examples.pronunciation: sore wa joudan dake nan da kara, nani mo sonnani okoru koto wa nai.

examples.japanese: 彼がその事件について何も知らないとは不思議だ。
examples.english: It is strange that he knows nothing about the matter.
examples.pronunciation: kare ga sono jiken ni tsuite nani mo shiranai to wa fushigi da.

examples.japanese: 見学旅行は先生もいるのだから、何も心配する必要はありませんよ。
examples.english: There are teachers on the tour, so you don't need to worry about anything.
examples.pronunciation: kengaku ryokou wa sensei mo iru no da kara, nani mo shinpai suru hitsuyou wa arimasen yo.

id: grammar:n2:全く～ない（まったく～ない）
word: 全く～ない（まったく～ない）
meaning: not at all ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: 全く + ... + ない
connectionSourcePoint: 全く～ない（まったく～ない）

examples.japanese: 全く漢字が書けない。
examples.english: I cannot write kanji at all.
examples.pronunciation: mattaku kanji ga kakenai.

examples.japanese: 今日は全く風がない。
examples.english: There is no wind today.
examples.pronunciation: kyou wa mattaku kaze ga nai.

examples.japanese: 私は彼の言うことがまったく理解できない。
examples.english: I can't understand what he says at all.
examples.pronunciation: watashi nwa kare no iu koto ga mattaku rikai deki nai.

examples.japanese: 彼が怒っている理由は、私にはまったくわからない。
examples.english: I have no idea why he is angry.
examples.pronunciation: kare ga okotte iru riyuu wa, watashi niwa mattaku wakara nai.

examples.japanese: もしかしたら彼とはまったく関係がないかもしれない。
examples.english: Maybe this hasn’t got anything to do with him at all.
examples.pronunciation: moshikashitara kare towa mattaku kankei ga nai kamoshirenai.

examples.japanese: 私の彼氏は、野球にはまったく興味がない。
examples.english: My boyfriend is not interested in baseball at all.
examples.pronunciation: watashi no kareshi wa, yakyuu ni wa mattaku kyoumi ga nai.

examples.japanese: 彼は強いです。どれだけ酒を飲んでも、まったく酔いがまわらなかった。
examples.english: He is a really strong drinker. No matter how much he drank, he didn't get drunk at all.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa tsuyoi desu. dore dake sake o non demo, mattaku yoi ga mawarana katta.

examples.japanese: 今の生活が大変で、将来のことはまったく考えられない。
examples.english: Life now is very difficult and I have no time to think about the future.
examples.pronunciation: ima ga taihen de, shourai no koto wa mattaku kangae rare nai.

examples.japanese: 彼らは初級クラスだが、全くの初心者ではない。
examples.english: They are in the beginner class, but they are no beginners.
examples.pronunciation: karera wa shokyuu kurasu daga, mattaku no shoshinsha de wa nai.

meaning: again; once more
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj 
再び ふたたび again; once more; a second time
来週 らいしゅう next week
月曜日 げつようび Monday
開始 かいし start; commencement; beginning; initiation​
今年 ことし this year
旅行 りょこう travel; trip; journey; excursion; tour
来年 らいねん next year
冬 ふゆ winter
来る くる to come
昨日 きのう yesterday
図書館 としょかん library
前 まえ previous; before; in front; ago
出会う であう to meet (by chance); to come across; to run across; to encounter
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 再び

examples.japanese: 来週の月曜日からレッスンを再び開始したいですか？
examples.english: Would you like to restart your lessons from next Monday?
examples.pronunciation: raishuu no getsuyoubi kara ressun o futatabi kaishi shitai desu ka?

examples.japanese: 今年の旅行は楽しかった。来年の冬再びここに来たい。
examples.english: This year's trip was fun. I want to come here again next winter.
examples.pronunciation: kotoshi no ryokou wa tanoshikatta. rainen no fuyu futatabi koko ni kitai.

examples.japanese: 10年後にようやく彼は、再び故郷の町を見た。
examples.english: He finally saw his hometown again after ten years.
examples.pronunciation: 10 nengo ni youyaku kare wa, futatabi kokyou no machi o mita.

examples.japanese: 昨日私は、図書館で１ヶ月前に出会った少女と再び会った。
examples.english: Yesterday I once again met the girl whom I met in the library a month ago.
examples.pronunciation: kinou watashi wa, toshokan de ikkagetsu mae ni deatta shoujo to futatabi atta.

examples.japanese: 本当に最悪の男だ。二度と再びあの男とは会いたくない。
examples.english: He really is the worst guy... I don't ever want to see him again.
examples.pronunciation: hontou ni saiaku no otoko da. nido to futatabi ano otoko to wa aitakunai.

examples.japanese: 彼女の両親が事故で亡くなってからピアノを弾かなかった。昨日は再び弾き始めた。
examples.english: She did not play the piano after her parents died in an accident. She started playing again yesterday.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo no ryoushin ga jiko de nakunatte kara piano o hikanakatta. kinou wa futatabi hiki hajimeta.

examples.japanese: 本当にいいの？ここを離れたら再び戻ることはできないよ。
examples.english: Are you sure about this? If you leave this place, you will never be able to come back.
examples.pronunciation: hontou ni ii no? koko o hanaretara futatabi modoru koto wa dekinai yo.

meaning: and; as well as ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: sentence / phrase + 及び + sentence / phrase

examples.japanese: 鉛筆及び紙を持ってきてください。
examples.english: Please bring a Pencil and a Paper.
examples.pronunciation: enpitsu oyobi kami o motte kite kudasai.

examples.japanese: 教室内での飲食及び喫煙はご遠慮ください。
examples.english: Please restrain from eating, drinking, and smoking within the classrooms.
examples.pronunciation: kyoushitsunai de no inshoku oyobi kitsuen wa go enryo kudasai.

examples.japanese: 安全のために観光する時、地図及びホテルの情報を持っていたほうがいい。
examples.english: To be safe when you are traveling, you should bring a map and your hotel information.
examples.pronunciation: anzen no tame ni kankou suru toki, chizu oyobi hoteru no jouhou o motte ita hou ga ii.

examples.japanese: 私は兄と外見及び性格が大きく異なる。
examples.english: There is a big difference in appearance and personality between me and my older brother.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa ani to gaiken oyobi seikaku ga ookiku kotonaru.

examples.japanese: 試験中は携帯電話および他の電子機器が使用禁止です。
examples.english: Mobile phones and other electronic devices are prohibited during the test.
examples.pronunciation: shikenchuu wa keitai denwa oyobi hoka no denshi kiki ga shiyou kinshi desu.

examples.japanese: 図書館の中では携帯電話及びゲーム機械を使ってはいけません。
examples.english: Do not use mobile phones or play games in the library.
examples.pronunciation: toshokan no nakade wa keitai denwa oyobi geemu kikai o tsukatte wa ikemasen.

examples.japanese: お祭りの前日および前々日は準備のため、休業させていただきます。
examples.english: We will be closed for preparation on both the day before and 2 days before the festival.
examples.pronunciation: omatsuri no zenjitsu oyobi zen zenjitsu wa junbi no tame, kyuugyou sasete itadakimasu.

examples.japanese: 山本氏は新聞および雑誌に多くのコラムを書いている。
examples.english: Mr. Yamamoto writes many columns for newspapers and magazines.
examples.pronunciation: yamamoto shi wa shinbun oyobi zasshi ni ooku no koramu o kaiteiru.

meaning: while, although; on the other hand~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: 普通形 + 反面
usageJapanese: 一方で / 一つの面では〜であるが、別の面から見ると

examples.japanese: この部屋は日当たりがいい反面、夏はかなり暑いです。
examples.english: This room has great light exposure, but on the other hand it is very hot in the summer.
examples.pronunciation: kono heya wa hiatari ga ii hanmen, natsu wa kanari atsui desu.

examples.japanese: 子供の成長は嬉しい反面、どこか寂しい。
examples.english: I'm happy to see my children grow, but on the other hand I am a little bit sad.
examples.pronunciation: kodomo no seichou wa ureshii hanmen, dokoka sabishii.

examples.japanese: 彼氏ができたら、楽しい反面、一人の時間がすくなくなりました。
examples.english: I'm happy to have a boyfriend, but on the other hand my time for myself has considerably dropped.
examples.pronunciation: kareshi ga dekitara, tanoshii hanmen, hitori no jikan ga sukunaku narimashita.

examples.japanese: 一人暮らしは楽しい反面、一緒に話す相手がいないので寂しいこともあります。
examples.english: Living alone is enjoyable, but on the other hand it can sometimes be a little lonely not having someone to talk to.
examples.pronunciation: hitori gurashi wa tanoshii hanmen, issho ni hanasu aite ga inai node sabishii koto mo arimasu.

examples.japanese: 子供にとって、インターネットは便利な反面、悪い影響を与えることもあります。
examples.english: For children, on one hand the inernet is very convenient, but there are also some negative effects as well.
examples.pronunciation: kodomo ni totte, intaanetto wa benri na hanmen, warui eikyou o ataeru koto mo arimasu.

examples.japanese: 転職をしたら、収入が増えた反面、ストレスも増えました。
examples.english: When changing jobs, although my salary increased, my stress also increased.
examples.pronunciation: tenshoku o shitara, shuunyuu ga fueta hanmen, sutoresu mo fuemashita.

examples.japanese: ノートパソコンは手軽である反面、壊れやすい。
examples.english: Laptops are cheap but on the other hand, they can break easily.
examples.pronunciation: nooto pasokon wa tegaru de aru hanmen, kowareyasui.

examples.japanese: 田舎暮らしは不便な反面、自然が多くて車が少ないので、快適に過ごしやすい。
examples.english: Although it is inconvenient to live in the countryside, there is a lot of nature and few cars, so it very pleasant for passing time.
examples.pronunciation: inaka kurashi wa fuben na hanmen, shizen ga ookute kuruma ga sukunai node, kaiteki ni sugoshi yasui.

id: grammar:n2:少しも～ない（すこしも～ない）
word: 少しも～ない（すこしも～ない）
meaning: not one bit; not even a little ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: 少しも + ... + ない
connectionSourcePoint: 少しも～ない（すこしも～ない）

examples.japanese: こんな暑い天気でピクニックするなんて、少しも楽しくないよ。
examples.english: Having a picnic in such hot weather isn't fun at all.
examples.pronunciation: konna atsui tenki de pikunikku suru nante, sukoshi mo tanoshikunai yo.

examples.japanese: この町は10年前と少しも変わらない。
examples.english: This town hasn't changed much from 10 years ago.
examples.pronunciation: kono machi wa 10 nen mae to sukoshi mo kawaranai.

examples.japanese: あなたは少しも日本語が読めないのですか。
examples.english: You can't read Japanese even a little?
examples.pronunciation: anata wa sukoshi mo nihongo ga yomenai no desu ka?

examples.japanese: 私は彼の言うことは少しも理解できなかった。
examples.english: I could not make any sense of what he said.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa kare no iu koto wa sukoshi mo rikai dekinakatta.

examples.japanese: そんな冗談は僕には少しも面白くないよ。
examples.english: That kind of joke isn't one bit funny to me.
examples.pronunciation: sonna joudan wa boku ni wa sukoshi mo omoshiro kunai yo.

examples.japanese: その薬は私には少しも効かなかった。
examples.english: The medicine had no effect on me.
examples.pronunciation: sono kusuri wa watashi ni wa sukoshi mo kikana katta.

examples.japanese: 彼が刑事になった事は少しも不思議でない。
examples.english: It's no surprise that he became a detective.
examples.pronunciation: kare ga keiji ni natta koto wa sukoshi mo fushigi de nai.

examples.japanese: 昨日の手術は彼女に少しも苦痛を与えない。
examples.english: She doesn't have any pain from her surgery yesterday.
examples.pronunciation: kinou no shujutsu wa kanojo ni sukoshi mo kutsuu o ataenai.

id: grammar:n2:少なくとも（すくなくとも）
word: 少なくとも（すくなくとも）
meaning: at least ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 少なくとも
connectionSourcePoint: 少なくとも（すくなくとも）

examples.japanese: 彼女は少なくとも３０歳だ。
examples.english: She is not less than thirty.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wa sukunaku tomo 30 sai da.

examples.japanese: 彼は少なくとも1日に10時間働いた。
examples.english: He worked for at least 10 hours a day.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa sukunaku tomo 1 nichi ni 10 jikan hataraita.

examples.japanese: 人数が足りませんね。少なくともあと3人は必要です。
examples.english: We are short on people. We need at least 3 more people.
examples.pronunciation: ninzuu ga tarimasen ne. sukunaku tomo ato 3 nin ha hitsuyou desu.

examples.japanese: あなたは少なくとも10時には寝るべきだ。
examples.english: You should go to bed at 10 o'clock at the very least.
examples.pronunciation: anata wa sukunaku tomo 10 ji ni wa neru beki da.

examples.japanese: 少なくとも週に１度は自分の部屋を掃除しなさい。
examples.english: Clean your room at least once a week.
examples.pronunciation: sukunaku tomo shuu ni 1 do wa jibun no heya o souji shinasai.

examples.japanese: その肉は少なくとも１時間は火にかける必要がある。
examples.english: The meat needs to cook for at least an hour.
examples.pronunciation: sono niku wa sukunaku tomo 1 jikan wa hi ni kakeru hitsuyou ga aru.

examples.japanese: 今一人暮らしをしていますが、毎月の生活費のうち、少なくとも5万円は食費で消えますね。外食が多いですから。
examples.english: Now I am living alone, but every month at least 50,000 yen of food expenses is added to my living expenses. This is because I often eat out.
examples.pronunciation: ima hitori gurashi o shiteimasu ga, maitsuki no seikatsuhi no uchi, sukunaku tomo 5 man en wa shokuhi de kiemasu ne. gaishoku ga ooi desu kara.

examples.japanese: 海外旅行に行くなら、現地の言葉のうち、少なくとも挨拶の表現ぐらいは覚えておいた方がいい。
examples.english: If you're going to travel abroad, you should at least memorize some greeting expressions of the local language.
examples.pronunciation: kaigai ryokou ni iku nara, genchi no kotoba no uchi, sukunaku tomo aisatsu no hyougen gurai wa oboete oita hou ga ii.

id: grammar:n2:幸いなことに（さいわいなことに）
word: 幸いなことに（さいわいなことに）
meaning: fortunately; luckily; thankfully ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 幸いなことに
connectionSourcePoint: 幸いなことに（さいわいなことに）

examples.japanese: 幸い彼は気づかなかった。
examples.english: Luckily, he did not notice.
examples.pronunciation: saiwai kare wa kizukanakatta.

examples.japanese: 幸い私は締切に間に合った。
examples.english: Fortunately, I was able to meet the deadline.
examples.pronunciation: saiwai watashi wa shimekiri ni ma ni atta.

examples.japanese: 幸いなことに、母の心臓の手術はうまくいった。
examples.english: Fortunately, my mother's heart surgery went well.
examples.pronunciation: saiwai na koto ni, haha no shinzou no shujutsu wa umaku itta.

examples.japanese: 幸いなことに、息子は新しい学校での生活にすぐに慣れた。
examples.english: Fortunately, my son quickly adjusted to life in his new school.
examples.pronunciation: saiwai na koto ni, musuko wa atarashii gakkou de no seikatsu ni sugu ni nareta.

examples.japanese: 幸いなことに私たちは事故を避けることができた。
examples.english: Fortunately, we were able to avoid an accident.
examples.pronunciation: saiwai na koto ni watashi tachi wa jiko o yokeru koto ga dekita

examples.japanese: 近所で火事が起きたが、その家の人たちは幸い無事だった。
examples.english: A fire broke out in the neighborhood, but luckily the people in that house were fine.
examples.pronunciation: kinjo de kaji ga okita ga, sono ie no hitotachi wa saiwai buji datta.

examples.japanese: 幸い大過なく仕事を終わらせることができた。
examples.english: Fortunately, I was able to finish my work without any serious errors.
examples.pronunciation: saiwai taika naku shigoto o owara seru koto ga dekita.

examples.japanese: 私はあなたのお役に立てれば幸いです。
examples.english: I am happy if I can help you.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa anata no o yakuni tatereba saiwai desu.

meaning: unable to; cannot; it is not possible to ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: V（ます形）ます + 得る / 得ない
usageJapanese: 〜できる / 〜できない
usageNotes:
①「能力的にできる・できない」の意味では使えない。
❌トムさんは少しだけ漢字を読み得る。
②「得る」は「える」と「うる」の２つの読み方があるが、否定形の場合は「えない」のみ。

examples.japanese: あの人が結婚したって本当！？え～！あり得ないよ！
examples.english: Is it true that he got married? No way! It's not possible!
examples.pronunciation: ano hito ga kekkon shitatte hontou! ? e~! ari enai yo!

examples.japanese: あの真面目な彼が犯人？そんなことはあり得ない。
examples.english: You think someone as earnest as him is the culprit? That's unbelievable..
examples.pronunciation: ano majime na kare ga hannin? sonna koto wa ari enai.

examples.japanese: 地震がいつ来るかなんて、予測し得ないことだ。
examples.english: It is unpredictable when an earthquake will occur.
examples.pronunciation: jishin ga itsu kuru ka nante, yosoku shi enai kotoda.

examples.japanese: 無から有は生じ得ない。
examples.english: Nothing can come of nothing.
examples.pronunciation: mu kara yuu wa shouji enai.

examples.japanese: 機械は完全には人力に代わり得ない。
examples.english: Machinery cannot completely take the place of human labor.
examples.pronunciation: kikai wa kanzen ni wa jinriki ni kawari enai.

examples.japanese: このような困難な仕事は、我々の力だけでは処理し得ない。
examples.english: These difficult tasks cannot be handled by our own power alone.
examples.pronunciation: kono youna konnan na shigoto wa, wareware no chikara dake dewa shori shi enai.

examples.japanese: 今回は予測し得ないことが起きたけど、皆さんは落ち着いてください。
examples.english: This time something completely unpredictable happened, but everyone please calm down.
examples.pronunciation: konkai wa yosoku shi enai koto ga okita kedo, minasan wa ochitsuite kudasai.

examples.japanese: 地震はいつ起こるか予想し得ないから、事前に家族との連絡方法や避難場所などを決めておいた方がいいでしょう。
examples.english: Since it is impossible to predict when an earthquake will occur, it is better to decide in advance how to contact your family and where to evacuate.
examples.pronunciation: jishin wa itsu okoru ka yosou shi enai kara, jizen ni kazoku to no renraku houhou ya hinan basho nado o kimete oita hou ga ii deshou.

meaning: can; to be able to; is possible to~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: V（ます形）ます + 得る / 得ない
usageJapanese: 〜できる / 〜できない
usageNotes:
①「能力的にできる・できない」の意味では使えない。
❌トムさんは少しだけ漢字を読み得る。
②「得る」は「える」と「うる」の２つの読み方があるが、否定形の場合は「えない」のみ。

examples.japanese: 君の話を信じるよ。だってこの世界ではどんなことでも起こり得るから。
examples.english: I believe in your story. I mean, anything is possible in this world.
examples.pronunciation: kimi no hanashi o shinjiru yo. datte kono sekaide wa donna koto demo okori eru kara.

examples.japanese: 事故はいつでも起こり得るので、気をつけてください。
examples.english: Accidents can happen at any time, so please be careful!
examples.pronunciation: jiko wa itsu demo okori eru node, ki o tsukete kudasai.

examples.japanese: タバコを吸いすぎや、お酒を飲み過ぎは病気の原因になり得ます。
examples.english: Excessive smoking and drinking too much alcohol can cause illness.
examples.pronunciation: tabako o sui sugi ya, o sake o nomi sugi wa byouki no genin ni nari emasu.

examples.japanese: 運動不足は病気の原因になり得るので、できるだけ体を動かすようにしてください。
examples.english: Lack of exercise can cause illness, so try to move as much as you can.
examples.pronunciation: undou fusoku wa byouki no genin ni nari eru node, dekiru dake karada o ugokasu youni shite kudasai.

examples.japanese: この不況では大手企業の倒産もあり得る。
examples.english: In this recession, it's possible some major companies may go bankrupt.
examples.pronunciation: kono fukyou dewa ootekigyou no tousan mo ariuru.

examples.japanese: 1つのミスが、会社を倒産に導くこともあり得ることだ。気を抜かずに、頑張っていこう。
examples.english: One mistake can cause a company to go bankrupt. Let's do our best without fail.
examples.pronunciation: hitotsu no misu ga, kaisha o tousan ni michibiku koto mo ari eru koto da. Ki o nukazu ni, ganbatte ikou.

examples.japanese: 混雑した地下鉄は、初めて乗る人々にとっては、とてもややこしいものとなり得る。
examples.english: Crowded subways can be very confusing for first-time users.
examples.pronunciation: konzatsu shita chikatetsu wa, hajimete noru hitobito ni totte wa, totemo yayakoshii mono to nari uru.

meaning: perhaps; likely; probably; I dare say ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 恐らく

examples.japanese: これは恐らく間違いです。
examples.english: This is likely a mistake.
examples.pronunciation: kore wa osoraku machigai desu.

examples.japanese: 彼は恐らくパーティーには来ないだろう。
examples.english: He probably won't come to the party.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa osoraku patii ni wa konai darou.

examples.japanese: 明日は恐らく雨が降るでしょう。
examples.english: It will rain perhaps tomorrow.
examples.pronunciation: ashita wa osoraku ame ga furu deshou.

examples.japanese: それはおそらく可能です。
examples.english: There might be a possibility.
examples.pronunciation: sore wa osoraku kanou desu.

examples.japanese: 恐らく彼は承知しないでしょう。
examples.english: I'm afraid he won't agree.
examples.pronunciation: osoraku kare wa shouchi shinai deshou.

examples.japanese: その事は恐らく君のいうとおりでしょう。
examples.english: I dare say you're right about that.
examples.pronunciation: sono koto wa osoraku kimi no iu toori deshou.

examples.japanese: 彼は35歳だと言うが，恐らく50歳近いだろう。
examples.english: He claims to be 35, but he is probably more like 50.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa 35 sai da to iu ga, osoraku 50 sai chikai darou.

id: grammar:n2:恐れがある（おそれがある）
word: 恐れがある（おそれがある）
meaning: it is feared that; to be in danger of; to be liable to ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: V（た形）+ 恐れがある
connectionSourcePoint: 恐れがある（おそれがある）

examples.japanese: 梅雨に入ってからほとんど雨が降っていない。このまま降らないと、水不足になる恐れがある。
examples.english: It has barely rained since entering the rainy season. If things continue like this, there is worry of a water shortage.
examples.pronunciation: tsuyu ni haitte kara hotondo ame ga futte inai. kono mama furanai to, mizubusoku ni naru osore ga aru.

examples.japanese: 大きい地震が来たら、この建物は倒れる恐れがある。
examples.english: If a big earthquake comes, this building may collapse.
examples.pronunciation: ookii jishin ga kitara, kono tatemono wa taoreru osore ga aru.

examples.japanese: この番組は子供に悪い影響を与える恐れがある。
examples.english: This show is worried to have a negative effect on children.
examples.pronunciation: kono bangumi wa kodomo ni warui eikyou o ataeru osore ga aru.

examples.japanese: 台風がこのまま北上すると、日本に上陸する恐れがある。
examples.english: If the typhoon continues north, it may land in Japan.
examples.pronunciation: taifuu ga kono mama hokujou suru to, nihon ni jouriku suru osore ga aru.

examples.japanese: このまま手術をしなければ、眼が見えなくなる恐れがあると医者に言われた。
examples.english: The doctor told me that if I didn't do the surgery, I could go blind.
examples.pronunciation: kono mama shujutsu o shinakereba, me ga mienaku naru osore ga aru to isha ni iwareta.

examples.japanese: こんなに赤字が続くと、この会社は倒産の恐れがある。
examples.english: If the deficit continues, the company is at risk of bankruptcy.
examples.pronunciation: konna ni akaji ga tsuduku to, kono kaisha wa tousan no osore ga aru.

examples.japanese: この原料は健康に害を与える恐れがあるので使用が禁止された。
examples.english: The use of this ingredient has been banned because it could be harmful to health.
examples.pronunciation: kono genryou wa kenkou ni gai o ataeru osore ga aru node shiyou ga kinshi sareta.

examples.japanese: イタリアのピザの斜塔は観光地として有名だが、そのまま何もしないと、倒れる恐れがあるということで、補強工事が行われた。
examples.english: Italy's Leaning Tower of Pisa is a famous tourist attraction, but it was feared to possibly fall over if nothing was done, so reinforcement construction was done.
examples.pronunciation: itaria no piza no shatou wa kankouchi toshite yuumei daga, sono mama nanimo shinai to, taoreru osore ga aru to iu koto de, hokyou kouji ga okonawareta.

id: grammar:n2:抜きにして / 抜きで（ぬきにして/ ぬきで）
word: 抜きにして / 抜きで（ぬきにして/ ぬきで）
meaning: without; leaving out; cutting out ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
Fixed expression / pattern: 抜きにして
Fixed expression / pattern: 抜きで（ぬきにして
Fixed expression / pattern: ぬきで）
connectionSourcePoint: 抜きにして / 抜きで（ぬきにして/ ぬきで）

examples.japanese: ハンバーガーはマスタード抜きでお願いします。
examples.english: No mustard on the hamburger please!
examples.pronunciation: hanbaaga wa masutaado nuki de onegai shimasu.

examples.japanese: 冗談抜きで真面目に考えってください。
examples.english: Stop joking around and think about it seriously.
examples.pronunciation: joudan nuki de majime ni kangaette kudasai.

examples.japanese: 今夜、仕事の話は抜きにして楽しく飲みましょう！
examples.english: Tonight, let's not talk about work and just enjoy ourselves!
examples.pronunciation: konya, shigoto no hanashi wa nuki ni shite tanoshiku nomimashou!

examples.japanese: すみません、チーズ抜きでお願いできますか。
examples.english: Excuse me, can I please have mine without cheese?
examples.pronunciation: sumimasen, chiizu nuki de onegai dekimasu ka.

examples.japanese: 毎回遅刻してくるし、今度のパーティーはトムさん抜きでやろうか。
examples.english: Since he always comes late, let's have the next party without Tom.
examples.pronunciation: maikai chikoku shitekuru shi, kondo no patii wa tomu-san nuki de yarou ka.

examples.japanese: 彼女抜きでは、このプロジェクトは成功しなかっただろう。
examples.english: Without her, this project would likely not have succeeded.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo nuki de wa, kono purojekuto wa seikou shinakatta darou.

meaning: to do something from beginning to end; completely, extremely ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 抜く

examples.japanese: 彼は、何においても頑張り抜く。
examples.english: He tries to do his best in everything.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa, nani ni oitemo ganbari nuku.

examples.japanese: あなたはこの仕事を最後までやり抜くことができますか。
examples.english: Can you stick to this job until it's completed?
examples.pronunciation: anata wa kono shigoto o saigo made yarinuku koto ga deki masu ka?

examples.japanese: 彼は決めたことは守り抜くでしょう。
examples.english: He will probably stick with the things he's decided on till the end.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa kimeta koto wa mamori nuku deshou.

examples.japanese: 大変な仕事でも、やり抜く自信があります。
examples.english: Even if the work is difficult, I have confidence to do it to the end.
examples.pronunciation: taihen na shigoto demo, yarinuku jishin ga arimasu.

examples.japanese: 彼は10年間雨の日も風の日もジョギングをやり抜いた。
examples.english: He's been jogging for 10 years, even on rainy or windy days.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa juunenkan ame no hi mo kaze no hi mo jogingu o yari nuita.

examples.japanese: フルマラソンはとても長く辛かったのですが、なんとか最後まで走り抜きました。
examples.english: The full marathon was very long and painful, but somehow I managed to finish running all the way until the end.
examples.pronunciation: furumarason wa totemo nagaku tsurakatta no desu ga, nanto ka saigo made hashiri nukimashita.

examples.japanese: 優勝するには、これから5つ試合を勝ち抜いていかなければならない。
examples.english: In order to achieve overall victory, we must win each of these 5 matches.
examples.pronunciation: yuushou suru niwa, kore kara itsutsu shiai o kachinuite ikanakereba naranai.

meaning: finally; after; following; at the end/ conclusion of ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
V（タ形） + 末に
Nの + 末に　※Nはする動詞のN
usageJapanese: 色々〜したけど、最終的に。
usageNotes: 末にの「に」は省略可能。

examples.japanese: いろいろ迷った末に、留学することにした。
examples.english: After worrying about various things, I decided to study abroad.
examples.pronunciation: iroiro mayotta sue ni, ryuugaku suru koto ni shita.

examples.japanese: 彼は3年間の闘病生活の末に、亡くなった。
examples.english: After fighting the illness for 3 years, he passed away.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa 3 nenkan no toubyouseikatsu no sue ni, naku natta.

examples.japanese: 考えた末に、会社を辞めて独立することにしました。
examples.english: After giving it a lot of thought, i decided to quit my job to start my own business.
examples.pronunciation: kangaeta sue ni, kaisha o yamete dokuritsu suru koto ni shimashita.

examples.japanese: 住民と話し合いの末、その建物の建設は中止となった。
examples.english: After the discussion with the residents, the construction of the building was suspended.
examples.pronunciation: juumin to no hanashiai no sue, sono tatemono no kensetsu wa chuushi to natta.

examples.japanese: ８時間にわたる話し合いの末に、やっと契約を結んだ。
examples.english: After discussing for 8 hours, we finally closed the deal.
examples.pronunciation: hachi jikan ni wataru hanashiai no sue ni, yatto keiyaku o musunda.

examples.japanese: 彼は色々と考えた末、大学を卒業したら国へ帰ることにした。
examples.english: After thinking about various things, he decided to return to his home country after graduating university.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa iroiro to kangaeta sue, daigaku o sotsugyou shi tara kuni e kaeru koto ni shita.

examples.japanese: 私は悩んだ末、進学ではなく就職することにした。
examples.english: After worrying about what to do, I decided to not continue my education and start working instead.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa nayanda sue, shingaku dewa naku shuushoku suru koto ni shita.

meaning: as was expected; sure enough; really; actually ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 果たして

examples.japanese: 果たしてそうだろうか。
examples.english: Can it really be so?
examples.pronunciation: hatashite sou darou ka.

examples.japanese: 果たして本当に足音が聞こえたのかどうかはわからない。
examples.english: I don’t know for sure if I really heard the footsteps or not.
examples.pronunciation: hatashite hontou ni ashioto ga kikoeta no ka dou ka wa wakaranai.

examples.japanese: そのうわさは果たして本当だろうか。
examples.english: Can that rumor really be true?
examples.pronunciation: sono uwasa wa hatashite hontou darou ka.

examples.japanese: 私があの店にいるとき、彼らがそこにやってきたのは果たして偶然だろうか？
examples.english: Was it really a coincidence that they came to the store while I was there?
examples.pronunciation: watashi ga ano mise ni iru toki, karera ga soko ni yatte kita no wa hatashite guuzen darou ka.

examples.japanese: この程度の金額で、果たして彼が承知するだろうか。
examples.english: Surely he will agree to this amount of money.
examples.pronunciation: kono teido no kingaku de, hatashite kare ga shouchi suru darou ka.

examples.japanese: 皆さんにも果たしていただく役割があります。
examples.english: Everyone surely has a role to carry out.
examples.pronunciation: minasan ni mo hatashite itadaku yakuwari ga arimasu.

examples.japanese: 機械には特に悪いところがないと、果たして何が故障の原因だったのだろうか。
examples.english: If there is really nothing wrong with the machine, then what was the cause of the failure?
examples.pronunciation: kikai niwa tokuni warui tokoro ga nai to, hatashite nani ga koshou no genin datta nodarou ka.

examples.japanese: 今度は果たして本当に見えたのかどうか自信がない。
examples.english: I can’t be sure that I’ve really seen it this time.
examples.pronunciation: kondo wa hatashite hontou ni mieta no ka dou ka jishin ga nai.

examples.japanese: この程度の補償金で、果たして被害者は納得するだろうか。
examples.english: Surely the victim will agree to this level of compensation payment.
examples.pronunciation: kono teido no hoshoukin de, hatashite higaisha wa nattoku suru darou ka.

meaning: depending on; so ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: N + 次第だ / 次第で
usageJapanese: 〜によって決まる

examples.japanese: 我々が成功できるかどうかは君次第だ。
examples.english: It is up to you whether we can succeed or not.
examples.pronunciation: wareware ga seikou dekiru ka douka wa kimi shidai da.

examples.japanese: 花火大会は天気次第で中止になる場合もあります。
examples.english: The fireworks festival may be cancelled depending on the weather.
examples.pronunciation: hanabi taikai wa tenki shidai de chuushi ni naru baai mo arimasu.

examples.japanese: 結婚した相手次第で人生が決まってしまうこともある。
examples.english: Sometimes the person you married will decide your future.
examples.pronunciation: kekkon shita aite shidai de jinsei ga kimatte shimau koto mo aru.

examples.japanese: ビアガーデンに客がたくさんいるかどうかは天気次第だ。
examples.english: The number of customers in the beer garden is at the mercy of the weather.
examples.pronunciation: biagaaden ni kyaku ga takusan iru ka douka wa tenki shidai da.

examples.japanese: 仕事は紹介するが、うまくいくかどうかは本人次第だ。
examples.english: I can introduce some work, but whether it goes well or not is dependent on the person interviewing.
examples.pronunciation: shigoto wa shoukai suru ga, umaku iku ka douka wa honnin shidai da.

examples.japanese: 言葉の使い方次第で、相手を喜ばせるもあるし、怒らせることもある。
examples.english: Depending on how you choose your words, you may either please or anger your partner.
examples.pronunciation: kotoba no tsukai kata shidai de, aite o yorokobaseru mo aru shi, okoraseru mo aru.

examples.japanese: この会社では年齢は関係ない。実力次第で年収もアップする。
examples.english: At this company, age is irrelevant. Depending on your ability, your annual salary will increase.
examples.pronunciation: kono kaisha de wa nenrei wa kankei nai. jitsuryoku shidai de nenshuu mo appu suru.

examples.japanese: 明日検査の結果が出ます。検査の結果次第では退院できるかもしれません。
examples.english: Tomorrow the test results will come out. Depending on the results, you may be able to leave the hospital.
examples.pronunciation: ashita kensa no kekka ga demasu. kensa no kekka shidai de taiin dekiru kamoshiremasen.

meaning: gradually (progress into a state); in sequence; in order
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 次第に

examples.japanese: 風は次第におさまった。
examples.english: The wind gradually died down.
examples.pronunciation: kaze wa shidai ni osamatta.

examples.japanese: それは次第に消えた。
examples.english: That gradually disappeared.
examples.pronunciation: sore wa shidai ni kieta.

examples.japanese: 彼らは次第に仲良くなっていった。
examples.english: They gradually became closer.
examples.pronunciation: karera wa shidai ni naka yoku natte itta.

examples.japanese: 今朝は雨でしたが、午後から次第に天気は回復してきました。
examples.english: It rained this mornin, but the weather gradually got better from the afternoon.
examples.pronunciation: kesa wa ame deshita ga, gogo kara shidai ni tenki wa kaifuku shite kimashita.

examples.japanese: 練習を続ければ、次第に結果が出るようになりますよ。
examples.english: If you keep practicing, you will gradually be able to get results.
examples.pronunciation: renshuu o tsuzukereba, shidai ni kekka ga deru you ni narimasu yo.

examples.japanese: 戦争によってその国は資源を次第に失った。
examples.english: The war gradually drained the country of its resources.
examples.pronunciation: sensou ni yotte sono kuni wa shigen o shidai ni ushinatta.

examples.japanese: 彼女は次第にこの田舎の生活に慣れてきている。
examples.english: She is gradually getting used to this countryside life.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wa shidai ni kono inaka no seikatsu ni narete kiteiru.

examples.japanese: 手当たり次第に本を読んだ。
examples.english: I read whatever I could get my hands on.
examples.pronunciation: teatari shidai ni hon o yonda.

meaning: as soon as, dependent upon
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Vます + 次第
usageJapanese: 〜したらすぐに / 〜が終わったらすぐに

examples.japanese: 部屋の準備ができ次第、会議を始めます。
examples.english: Once the room preparations are complete, we will begin the meeting.
examples.pronunciation: heya no junbi ga deki shidai, kaigi o hajime masu.

examples.japanese: 雨がやみ次第、出発することにしましょう。
examples.english: Let's depart once the rain stops.
examples.pronunciation: ame ga yami shidai, shuppatsu suru koto ni shimashou.

examples.japanese: 詳しいことは情報が入り次第、お伝えします。
examples.english: I will inform you with more specific details once I get more information.
examples.pronunciation: kuwashii koto wa jouhou ga hairi shidai, otsutae shimasu.

examples.japanese: 式が終了次第、ロビーに集合してください。
examples.english: As soon as the ceremony is over, please gather in the lobby.
examples.pronunciation: shiki ga shuuryou shidai, robii ni shuugou shite kudasai.

examples.japanese: 来週のスケジュールが決まり次第、連絡してください。
examples.english: Please contact me as soon as next week’s schedule is decided.
examples.pronunciation: raishuu no sukejuuru ga kimarishidai, renraku shite kudasai.

examples.japanese: 参加者の情報がわかり次第、教えていただけませんか。
examples.english: Once you know the data for the participants, would you please let me know?
examples.pronunciation: sankasha no jouhou ga wakarishidai, oshiete itadakemasen ka.

examples.japanese: 議長が到着次第、会議を始めたいと思いますので、もうしばらくお待ちください。
examples.english: We will start the meeting once the chairman arrives, so please wait a little while longer.
examples.pronunciation: gichou ga touchaku shidai, kaigi o hajimetai to omoimasu node, mou shibaraku omachi kudasai.

examples.japanese: 見つかり次第、ご連絡差し上げますから、ここに連絡先の電話番号を書いてください。
examples.english: I will contact you once I find it, so please write your contact phone number here.
examples.pronunciation: mitsukari shidai, gorenraku sashiagemasu kara, koko ni renrakusaki no denwa bangou o kaite kudasai.

id: grammar:n2:甲斐がある（かいがある）
word: 甲斐がある（かいがある）
meaning: it’s worth one’s efforts to do something
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: V（た形）+ 甲斐がある
connectionSourcePoint: 甲斐がある（かいがある）

examples.japanese: 努力の甲斐があって、希望の大学に合格した。
examples.english: It was worth working so hard. I got into my target university.
examples.pronunciation: doryoku no kai ga atte, kibou no daigaku ni goukaku shita.

examples.japanese: 美味しいと言ってたくさん食べてくれると、頑張って作った甲斐があります。
examples.english: When someone says it is delicious and eats a lot, I feel it was worth working hard making it.
examples.pronunciation: oishii to itte takusan tabete kureru to, ganbatte tsukutta kai ga arimasu.

examples.japanese: 毎日ダイエットを頑張った甲斐があって、５キロ痩せることができました。
examples.english: It was worth working hard on my diet every day, as I've managed to lose 5 kilograms.
examples.pronunciation: mainichi daietto o ganbatta kai ga atte, 5 kiro yaseru koto ga dekimashita.

examples.japanese: 毎日練習した甲斐があって、日本語が上手にできるようになった。
examples.english: It was worth practicing everyday as I was able to become good at Japanese.
examples.pronunciation: mainichi renshuu shita kai ga atte, nihongo ga jouzu ni dekiru youni natta.

examples.japanese: お客さんに喜んでもらえると、この仕事は、やり甲斐があると思う。
examples.english: When a customer is happy, I feel that this job is worth doing.
examples.pronunciation: okyaku san ni yorokonde moraeru to, kono shigoto wa, yari gai ga aru to omou.

examples.japanese: 毎日つまらない仕事ばかりしている。もっと働き甲斐がある会社に転職したい。
examples.english: Everyday I continue to do the same boring job. I want to change jobs to a company with work worth doing.
examples.pronunciation: mainichi tsumaranai shigoto bakari shiteiru. motto hataraki gai ga aru kaisha ni tenshoku shitai.

examples.japanese: こんなに美味しいラーメンが食べられるなら、２時間も列に並んだ甲斐があると思う。
examples.english: If you can eat ramen that is this good, it is worth waiting in line 2 hours for.
examples.pronunciation: konna oishii raamen ga taberareru nara, 2 jikan mo retsu ni naranda kai ga aru to omou.

meaning: at once; immediately; directly; in person; automatically ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 直ちに

examples.japanese: 彼らは直ちに彼女に手術をしなければならないだろう。
examples.english: They will have to operate on the woman immediately.
examples.pronunciation: karera wa tadachi ni kanojo ni shujitsu o shinakereba naranai darou.

examples.japanese: 私はこの仕事を直ちにしなければならない。
examples.english: I must do this work immediately.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa kono shigoto o tadachi ni shinakereba naranai.

examples.japanese: 万一間違いを見つけたら、直ちに知らせてください。
examples.english: If you should find any mistakes, please let me know at once.
examples.pronunciation: manichi machigai o mitsuketara, tadachi ni shirasete kudasai.

examples.japanese: 彼女は仕事が終わって直ちに事務所を出た。
examples.english: She left the office immediately after work.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wa shigoto ga owatte tadachi ni jimusho o deta.

examples.japanese: 直ちに出発しないと、私は約束に遅れるだろう。
examples.english: Unless I leave right away, I'll be late for my appointment.
examples.pronunciation: tadachi ni shuppatsu shinai to, watashi wa yakusoku ni okureru darou.

examples.japanese: 彼の様子はよくないから、直ちに病院に連れていこう！
examples.english: He doesn't look good, so let's take him to the hospital right away.
examples.pronunciation: kare no yousu wa yokunai kara, tadachi ni byouin ni tsurete ikou!

examples.japanese: 彼らはその計画を直ちに実行に移すべきだと提案した。
examples.english: They suggested that we should put the plan into practice right now.
examples.pronunciation: karera wa sono keikaku o tadachi ni jikkou ni utsusu beki da to teian shita.

examples.japanese: 新型コロナウイルスに感染した人は直ちに隔離された。
examples.english: Individuals infected with the new coronavirus were immediately quarantined.
examples.pronunciation: shingata korona uirusu ni kansen shita hito wa tadachi ni kakuri sareta.

meaning: in short; in a word; to sum up; to put it simply ~
related:
paraphrisation "in other words", say something in own words, reinterpretation:
わけだ
つまり
すなわち
要するに
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: 要するに

examples.japanese: 要するに時と金の問題だ。
examples.english: In short, it is a question of time and money.
examples.pronunciation: you suru ni toki to kane no mondai da.

examples.japanese: 要するに、彼女は行きたくないって言ったでしょう。
examples.english: To sum up, she said she doesn't want to go, right?
examples.pronunciation: you suru ni, kanojo wa ikitakunaitte itta deshou.

examples.japanese: その会合は勉強にならない。要するに時間の無駄だった。
examples.english: That gathering did not help with my studies, in short it was a waste of time.
examples.pronunciation: sono kaigou wa benkyou ni naranai. you suru ni jikan no muda datta.

examples.japanese: 終電が出てしまった。要するに、今日は電車で帰れないということだ。
examples.english: The last train left.. In short, I won't be able to return home by train today.
examples.pronunciation: shuuden ga dete shimatta. you suru ni, kyou wa densha de kaerenai toiu koto da.

examples.japanese: 合格点に10点足りず、不合格でした。要するに、まだまだ努力が足りなかった。
examples.english: I was short 10 points of a passing score and failed. In short, I did not put in enough effort..
examples.pronunciation: goukakuten ni 10 ten tarizu, fugoukaku deshita. you suru ni, mada mada doryoku ga tarinakatta.

examples.japanese: 彼は一度も入賞しなかった。要するに才能がなかったということだ。
examples.english: He's never even once placed in a contest. To put it simply, lacked talent.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa ichido mo nyuushou shinakatta. you suru ni, sainou ga nakatta toiu koto da.

examples.japanese: 試合に大負けした。要するに、相手のチームと力の差があったということだ。
examples.english: We had a crushing defeat in the match. To put it simply, there was a big gap of strength between the opposing team.
examples.pronunciation: shiai ni oomake shita. you suru ni, aite no chiimu to chikara no sa ga atta toiu koto da.

examples.japanese: 今日のスピーチは緊張して間違えてしまいました。要するに、練習不足ということです。
examples.english: In today's speach, I was nervous and made a mistake. In short, I lacked in practice.
examples.pronunciation: kyou no supiichi wa kinchou shite machigaete shimai mashita. you suru ni, renshuu fusoku toiu koto desu.

meaning: conversely; on the contrary ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: N / phrase / sentence + 逆に

examples.japanese: 娘はスポーツが好きだが、逆に息子はスポーツが嫌いだ。
examples.english: My daughter likes sports, but on the other hand my son hates them.
examples.pronunciation: musume wa supootsu ga suki da ga, gyaku ni musuko wa supootsu ga kirai da.

examples.japanese: 天気予報と逆に、雨が降り続いている。
examples.english: It’s been raining nonstop in contrast to the weather forecast.
examples.pronunciation: tenki yohou to gyaku ni, ame ga furitsuzuite iru.

examples.japanese: 彼はゲームが大好きだが、逆に弟さんは嫌いだ。
examples.english: He loves to play games, but contrarily his younger brother dislikes them.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa geemu ga daisuki daga, gyaku ni otouto san wa kirai da.

examples.japanese: 逆に、嫌いな日本の料理はなんですか？
examples.english: On the other hand, what is a Japanese food that you hate?
examples.pronunciation: gyaku ni, kirai na nihon no ryouri wa nan desu ka?

examples.japanese: 私達の期待とは逆に、今年の売り上げは増えなかった。
examples.english: Contrary to our expectations, sales did not increase this year.
examples.pronunciation: watashitachi no kitai to wa gyaku ni, kotoshi no uriage wa fuenakatta.

examples.japanese: 私はストレスがたまると食欲がなくなるではなく、逆に何か食べないと気が済まなくなる。
examples.english: I don't lose my appetite when I get stressed, rather I can't calm down unless I eat something.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa sutoresu ga tamaru to shokuyoku ga nakunarude wa naku, gyaku ni nanika tabenaito ki ga sumanaku naru.

examples.japanese: 今回の試験は自信が満々で合格できると思っていたけど、逆に落ちた。
examples.english: I was full of confidence that I could pass this exam, but I ended up failing.
examples.pronunciation: konkai no shiken wa jishin ga manman de goukaku dekiru to omotte ita kedo, gyaku ni ochita.

examples.japanese: 薬によっては、役に立つどころか逆に害になるものもある。
examples.english: Some medicines do more harm than good.
examples.pronunciation: kusuri ni yotte wa, yaku ni tatsu dokoro ka gyaku ni gai ni naru mono mo aru.

meaning: as long as; while… is the case; as far as; while ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
V（辞書形） + かぎり
Nの + かぎり
usageJapanese: 〜の限界まで / できるところのギリギリまで

examples.japanese: 明日は、雨が降らない限り、10時に学校で会いましょう。
examples.english: As long as it doesn't rain tomorrow, let's meet at the school at 10 am.
examples.pronunciation: ashita wa, ame ga furanai kagiri, 10 ji ni gakkou de aimashou.

examples.japanese: この仕事は、私が生きている限り、ずっと続けていきたい。
examples.english: I want to continue doing this job as long as I am alive.
examples.pronunciation: kono shigoto wa, watashi ga ikiteiru kagiri, zutto tsudukete ikitai.

examples.japanese: 日本にいる限り、日本語が必要だよ。
examples.english: While you are in Japan, you need to know Japanese (language).
examples.pronunciation: nihon ni iru kagiri, nihongo ga hitsuyou da yo.

examples.japanese: 今のやり方を変えない限り、悪い状況は続くでしょう。
examples.english: As long the current way of doing things doesn't change, these bad conditions will likely continue.
examples.pronunciation: ima no yarikata o kaenai kagiri, warui joukyou wa tsuduku deshou.

examples.japanese: お店の中にお客様がいる限り、お店は閉めません。
examples.english: While there are still customers inside, this shop will not close.
examples.pronunciation: omise no naka ni okyakusama ga iru kagiri, omise wa shime masen.

examples.japanese: 悪い生活習慣を改めない限り、健康にはなれない。
examples.english: As long as you don't fix your bad lifestyle habits you will not be able to get healthy.
examples.pronunciation: warui seikatsu shuukan o aratamenai kagiri, kenkou niwa narenai.

examples.japanese: 自分の歌を聞いてくれる人がいる限り、路上で歌たい続けようと思います。
examples.english: As long as there are people listening to my songs, I plan to continue singing.
examples.pronunciation: jibun no uta o kiite kureru hito ga iru kagiri, rojou de utai tsudukeyou to omoi masu.
